title: Intellia Therapeutics Reports Positive Phase 3 Results in Hereditary Angioedema, Marking Global First for In Vivo Gene Editing
text: Intellia Therapeutics announced positive topline results from the global Phase 3 HAELO clinical trial of lonvo-z (formerly NTLA-2002) in hereditary angioedema (HAE). The Phase III HAELO trial met its primary and all key secondary endpoints, showing an 87% reduction in mean monthly attacks (0.26 vs 2.1) and 62% of lonvo-z patients were attack-free versus 11% on placebo. Safety was favorable, with only mild to moderate adverse events and no serious events reported in the treatment arm. Intellia initiated a rolling BLA submission to the FDA and is preparing for a potential U.S. launch of lonvo-z in the first half of 2027. A total of 80 patients were enrolled, with 52 receiving lonvo-z and 28 receiving placebo, with 49% of patients enrolled in the United States and 71% on long-term prophylaxis therapy at study entry. This represents the first Phase 3 data for an in vivo CRISPR gene editing candidate globally, marking a historic milestone in the field.
